{{Quote|Me, a Jedi? No dear lad, you have seen far too many a drama I think.|Damien Tantrellius|<ref name="pob" />}}
'''Damien Tantrellius''' was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. Following an incident that resulted in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of future-[[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Zannen]]'s wife, Tantrellius lost his memory, and subsequently took up a new career as an actor. This allowed him to escape the [[Great Jedi Purge/Legends|Great Jedi Purge]], as even he was unaware of his identity and power. Tantrellius rose to fame playing the lead in ''[[Jedi Men From Baronis]]'', and so he formed the [[Tantrellius Theatre Groupe]].

With his daughter, [[Plum Tantrellius|Plum]], [[Yoh Podd]] and [[Annah Dublavviane]], Tantrellius and his company of players eked out an existence on the canal [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Peekoine]], in the mobile [[City/Legends|city]] of [[Landslasher-4]]. Though he did not know it, Tantrellius would hold his audiences in a trance-like state with [[The Force/Legends|the Force]], adding to his performance. During the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], Plum was briefly kidnapped by a [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]], but was rescued by [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel Alliance]] agents.

==Biography==
===Clone Wars===
The man who would come to be known as Damien Tantrellius was a [[Jedi Knight/Legends|Jedi Knight]] who served during the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]]. During a particular battle, Tantrellius' actions resulted in the [[Death/Legends|death]] of a woman married to future-[[Governor/Legends|Governor]] [[Zannen]]. The trauma surrounding the experience resulted in memory loss for Tantrellius, and he completely forgot about his [[Jedi/Legends|Jedi]] heritage. He took on the name "Damien Tantrellius," as he had no recollection of his former name either.<ref name="pob" />

===Tantrellius Theatre Groupe===
{{Quote|Isn't Damien absolutely ma-a-ahvelous tonight, dahlings?|Annah Dublavviane|<ref name="pob" />}}
[[File:Bespin EotECR.png|thumb|left|250px|Tantrellius rose to fame on Bespin after becoming an actor.]]
Becoming an actor, Tantrellius formed the [[Tantrellius Theatre Groupe]]. He rose to fame on<ref name="pob" /> the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]]<ref name="wizards">{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20040930bespinmain|text=Bespin: Action Tidings|int=Bespin: Action Tidings}}</ref> [[Gas giant/Legends|gas giant]]<ref name="CL">[[Star Wars: Complete Locations (2005)|''Star Wars: Complete Locations'']]</ref> [[Bespin/Legends|Bespin]] performing in ''[[Jedi Men From Baronis]]'', playing, aptly enough, a Jedi Knight. Tantrellius also had a daughter, [[Plum Tantrellius|Plum]], to look after. Plum deeply admired her father, and sought to follow in his footsteps as a thespian. Also in Tantrellius' acting troupe were [[Yoh Podd]] and [[Annah Dublavviane]].<ref name="pob" />

At the conclusion of the Clone Wars, [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine]] made himself [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]], transforming the [[Galactic Republic/Legends|Galactic Republic]] into an [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]]. The Jedi were [[Declaration of a New Order|declared]] enemies of the state, and were summarily [[Order 66/Legends|hunted down]] and executed.<ref name="rots">{{Film|III}}</ref> Tantrellius, in his new role as an actor, was able to evade death simply through ignorance, as most people did not know that he was in fact a Jedi, least of all himself. Relocating to<ref name="pob" /> the [[Raithal sector]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]]<ref name="VSF13">{{VoyagesCite|13|Twinkle, Twinkle Little Star, How I Wonder Where We Are}}</ref> [[Peekoine]], Tantrellius performed at the [[Jediboh Theatre]] on [[Landslasher-4]], a floating [[City/Legends|city]] that doubled as a canal cutter. Following the formation of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], Tantrellius reprised his role from ''Jedi Men from Baronis'' in the sequel, ''[[Two Jedi Men From Baronis]]''. Requesting clearance from Peekoine's governor, none other than Zannen, Tantrellius participated in a particularly tense conversation with the Imperial over a meal. Following the discussion, Tantrellius was allowed to stage the play.<ref name="pob" />

He received rave reviews in the role from [[The Slasher Sun Times]] critic [[Rojene Eborsisk]], who called the performance "mesmerizing." Plum made her debut in ''Two Jedi Men From Baronis'', playing the character of [[Leia (character)|Leia]] to acclaim. During his performance, Tantrellius would inadvertently use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to captivate his audience, keeping them in an almost trance-like state. He would occasionally remember elements of his previous Jedi life, but would attribute the flashbacks to his immersion in the role.<ref name="pob" />

During a performance of ''Two Jedi Men From Baronis'', Governor Zannen and his daughter, [[Yuma Zannen]], were present. While on stage, Tantrellius was met by a group of agents from the Rebel Alliance, who informed him that Plum had gone missing. Deeply concerned, Tantrellius began searching through official channels for a ransom message, while sending the Rebels, as well as Plum's friend, [[Grree L'Tharr]], and Podd to find his daughter. The Rebels returned with the information that Plum had been mistaken for Yuma Zannen. Yuma had arranged to be "kidnapped" by the criminal [[Gamusha]]'s [[Swoop gang/Legends|swoop gang]], [[The Gutterwipes]], in an effort for attention, but the plan had backfired.<ref name="pob" />

==Personality and traits==
Tantrellius spent most of his time on the stage, even when there was no performance in progress. He was a deep thinker, although he was prone to panic in times of stress. He was admired by critics and colleagues alike, though his greatest fan was his own daughter, Plum. Tantrellius would immerse himself in his roles, so much so that he thought his subconscious was starting to believe that he actually ''was'' a Jedi. What complicated matters further was that he genuinely had a connection to the Force, and was a Jedi in the Galactic Republic's heyday.<ref name="pob" />

==Behind the scenes==
Damien Tantrellius first appeared in [[William Herz]]'s [[1990]] roleplaying adventure ''[[Peekoine Opera Blues]]'', which was published in ''[[Space Gamer/Fantasy Gamer 88]]''. In the story, Tantrellius serves as a supporting non-player character. As the adventure was released outside of the [[Lucas Licensing]] process,<ref name="pob" /> it is considered non-[[canon]]ical with respect to the [[Star Wars Legends|''Star Wars'' Legends]] continuity.<ref name="UnlicensedStatus">{{UnlicensedStatus}}</ref>
